data.title: Everlight Solar named Best Solar Provider 2025 in Nebraska's Cream of the Crop Awards for Technology in Agriculture.
data.description: In Omaha, Nebraska on September 26, 2025, Everlight Solar was announced as the recipient of the Cream of the Crop award in the Technology in Agriculture category, showcasing their prominent role in promoting clean energy innovations. The award highlights the company's dedication to advancing sustainable energy solutions. The Cream of the Crop Awards recognize the top achievers in Nebraska's various sectors.

data.title: Learn about prediction markets from experts like Scott Duke Kominers, Alex Tabarrok, and Sonal Chokshi. Dive into design, functionality, and applications.
data.description: Join a discussion led by experts Scott Duke Kominers, Alex Tabarrok, and Sonal Chokshi to delve into the details of prediction markets. Gain insights into their structure, features, and possible uses in various fields. Click to find out more about these markets.

data.title: Ex-SEC commissioner plans NFT ventures; aims to improve crypto policy, clarifies earlier joke about creating NFTs post role.
data.description: The commissioner, who initially joked about "becoming an NFT creator" post her tenure at the SEC, later implied her focus on ensuring the formulation of sound crypto policies.

data.title: Pro Padel League's City’s Cup Finals will be at Hammerstein Ballroom in NYC from October 16-19. Tickets start at $30. VIP at $190.
data.description: The Pro Padel League (PPL) revealed today that the 2025 season championship, the City’s Cup Finals, is set to take place at the prestigious Hammerstein Ballroom in New York City from Thursday, October 16, to Sunday, October 19. Tickets for the four-day championship tournament are currently available for purchase on Ticketmaster. General admission tickets are priced from $30 onwards, while VIP tickets, which offer prime seating and access to private lounges, start at $190.

data.title: Maxus Mining consolidates Quarry Project with Beveley Property acquisition in British Columbia, comprising 108 hectares.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia on September 26, 2025, Maxus Mining Inc. has signed a Mineral Claims Purchase Agreement with John Nick Bakus to buy the Beveley Property located in northern British Columbia, Canada. The agreement states that Maxus will acquire a 100% interest in the Property, which includes two mineral claims totaling 108 hectares within the Quarry Project area. This acquisition is significant as it adds known critical metal mineralization and a historical NI 43-101 non-compliant mineral resource to the company's portfolio. The Property purchase agreement was made with an arms-length third party and will enhance Maxus Mining Inc.'s presence in the region.

data.title: Huntsman Corp (NYSE: HUN) announces Julia Wright's withdrawal as Executive VP, GC, & Secretary due to personal reasons.
data.description: In The Woodlands, Texas, on September 26, 2025, Huntsman Corporation (NYSE: HUN) revealed that Julia Wright, appointed as the new Executive Vice President, General Counsel, and Secretary succeeding David Stryker on September 4, has decided to step down from the role citing personal reasons.

data.title: Omnicom and IPG finalize agreement with FTC after public comment period.
data.description: On September 26, 2025, Omnicom Group Inc. ("Omnicom") (NYSE: OMC) and The Interpublic Group of Companies, Inc. ("IPG") (NYSE: IPG) revealed that the United States Federal Trade Commission has concluded its agreement following a public comment period.

data.title: Unit Corporation to re-balance board with Steven B. Hildebrand not standing for re-election. Alan J. Carr resigns.
data.description: TULSA, Okla.-- Unit Corporation disclosed that Steven B. Hildebrand will not seek re-election at the 2025 annual stockholders' meeting. To maintain the Company's charter requirements regarding the balanced composition of directors, Alan J. Carr also resigned as a Group 2 Director. This decision ensures that the Group 1 and Group 2 directors remain nearly equal in number. The move was made to uphold the mandate that the two groups should each comprise about half of the overall director count. These changes are part of the ongoing efforts to maintain a balanced and effective leadership structure within the company.

data.title: Lockheed Martin Sikorsky gets $10.855 billion contract to build up to 99 CH-53K® helicopters for USMC, ensuring steady deliveries.
data.description: A new multi-year contract worth $10.855 billion has been awarded to Sikorsky, a subsidiary of Lockheed Martin (NYSE: LMT), by the U.S. Navy on September 26, 2025. This contract is aimed at stabilizing the U.S. industrial base and ensuring consistent deliveries of aircraft. Sikorsky will be responsible for building a maximum of 99 CH-53K® helicopters under this agreement.

data.title: Public Storage subsidiary, PSOC, priced a public offering of €425M in senior notes due 2034, guaranteed by the Company, with 3.5% interest.
data.description: Public Storage (NYSE:PSA) based in Glendale, California, revealed that its subsidiary, Public Storage Operating Company (PSOC), has established the pricing for a public offering of €425 million in total principal amount for senior notes that will reach maturity in 2034. These notes will be secured by the Company and will accrue interest at a rate of 3.500% per year, being issued at 99.447% of their par value. The annual interest on the notes will be payable, and the official maturity date for these notes is January 20, 2034.

data.title: Scott Alexander Rose recognized as a Pinnacle Achiever 2025 by The Inner Circle for contributions in Insurance, Education, and Philanthropy.
data.description: In Boca Raton, Florida on September 26, 2025, Scott Alexander Rose was recognized in The Inner Circle as a Pinnacle Achiever 2025 for his impactful work in Insurance, Education, and Philanthropy. Dr. Rose's dedication to education and community stands out prominently in this acknowledgment.

data.title: Wendy Poage named Inner Circle Lifetime Member in Healthcare & Patient Advocacy. Dynamic career in Healthcare.
data.description: On September 26, 2025, in Elizabeth, Colorado, Wendy Poage, MHA, was recognized in The Inner Circle as a Lifetime Member for her significant impact on Healthcare and Patient Advocacy. With a distinguished career in healthcare, Wendy Poage, MHA, has made notable contributions to the field.

data.title: David Reynolds Thrasher, MD recognized as Pinnacle Professional Member of The Inner Circle for achievements in Pulmonary Medicine and Community Service.
data.description: In Montgomery, Alabama on September 26, 2025, Dr. David Reynolds Thrasher was celebrated as a distinguished member of The Inner Circle and recognized as a Pinnacle Professional Member for his outstanding work in Pulmonary Medicine and Community Service. Dr. Thrasher's achievements were highlighted in a prestigious publication, honoring his significant contributions to these fields.

data.title: Jade A Starry named Pinnacle Professional Member of Inner Circle for Maternal/Newborn Care contributions.
data.description: In Lewiston, Idaho on September 26, 2025, Jade A. Starry was recognized in The Inner Circle as a Pinnacle Professional Member Inner Circle of Excellence for her notable work in Maternal and Newborn Care. With a successful career in this field, Jade A. Starry has been acknowledged for her contributions.

data.title: Jewel Montgomery Smay, MD recognized as a Pinnacle Professional Member in Leading Pediatric Anesthesiology in The Inner Circle.
data.description: In Tulsa, Oklahoma on September 26, 2025, Dr. Jewel Montgomery Smay, MD was recognized in The Inner Circle as a distinguished Pinnacle Professional Member for her exceptional work in Pediatric Anesthesiology. She was praised for her expertise and compassion in leading in this field.

Ancient skull from China may shake up timeline of human evolution A skull found in China could shake up the understanding of how human evolution unfolded over the past million years and paleoanthropologists in China and the UK are getting excited. It’s all down to new studies

data.title: Silvercorp Metals Inc. releases 2025 AGM results with TSX/NYSE symbol SVM, all matters approved by shareholders.
data.description: SVM, a mining company listed on both the Toronto Stock Exchange and NYSE American, released positive news on September 26, 2025. In the report, Silvercorp Metals Inc. announced that all resolutions set out in the Company's Notice of Meeting were successfully approved by the shareholders. The company's trading symbols on both exchanges are TSX:SVM and NYSE American: SVM.
Fitch Ratings: Spain strong labor force growth reflects major migration inflows, mostly from Latin America

data.title: Citi predicts stablecoin market cap to hit $4T by 2030, based on a $1.9T projection for 2025.
data.description: Analysts at Citi have projected significant growth in the stablecoin market into the future. They anticipate a base case scenario reaching $1.9 trillion by 2025 and a bull case scenario reaching $4 trillion by 2030. This upward trend suggests a substantial increase in the value and usage of stablecoins in the coming years.

data.title: Emmis Acquisition Corp. closes $115M IPO with units including 1 ordinary share & share right for 1/10th of a share.
data.description: Each unit consists of one Class A ordinary share and one share right to receive 1/10th of a Class A ordinary share, totaling two securities per unit.
